cover_story.headline: Haaland Is Quietly Building a Tech Empire From Norway
cover_story.dek: Erling Haaland scores for Manchester City and invests in health and performance tech startups from his home country — building a portfolio designed to outlast his boots.
cover_story.author: Ingrid Solberg
body: Erling Haaland is 25, scores more goals per game than any striker in Premier League history, and has spent the last two years building Nine Holdings — a Norwegian-registered investment vehicle managing a growing portfolio of health, performance, and consumer technology companies. The fund is headquartered in Bryne, the small town on Norway's west coast where Haaland grew up, and operates with a three-person team of investment and legal professionals.

Nine Holdings has made eight confirmed investments since 2024. The anchor position is a reported NOK 42M stake in Athlean Health, an Oslo-based biomarker monitoring platform that tracks recovery, inflammation, and load data for elite sports clubs. Athlean raised NOK 180M in a Series A in February 2026 and now works with 14 clubs across the Norwegian, Danish, and Swedish top flights. Haaland's other holdings include a seed position in Vessel — a Norwegian DTC hydration and electrolyte brand — and a minority stake in data analytics platform FieldIQ, which is currently piloting with four Premier League academies.

The strategic logic is deliberate. Every company in the Nine Holdings portfolio connects to performance science, recovery, or athlete data — areas Haaland has personal expertise in and where he can provide credible commercial support. The fund's stated next step is a €15M raise in late 2026 to take Nine Holdings from a personal vehicle to a formal fund structure, with third-party LPs joining alongside Haaland's anchor capital.
cover_story.quote: I invest in things I understand from the inside. If I've used it, tested it, and it works — that's the diligence. Nobody knows performance science better than the people who live it.
cover_story.stat_label: Target raise for Nine Holdings fund conversion — Q4 2026

tips.headline: Invest in Industries You Live Inside
tips.body: Your lived experience as a professional athlete is diligence no analyst can replicate. When evaluating a company in nutrition, recovery, or performance data — your first-hand knowledge is a genuine edge. Prioritise sectors where you can add credibility, not just capital.

tips.headline: Register Your Name as a Trademark Now
tips.body: Your name and number are commercial assets. File trademark applications in your home country and key markets before anyone else does — the cost is minimal, the upside is permanent. Haaland's 'Nine' branding is protected IP, not a coincidence.

tips.headline: Keep Your Base of Operations Close to Home
tips.body: Running your investment vehicle from your home country reduces legal complexity, builds local networks, and keeps tax structures clean. Operating from a jurisdiction you understand is worth more than a headline address in London or New York.

tips.headline: Anchor Your Portfolio With One Conviction Position
tips.body: A single large, high-conviction bet — like Haaland's NOK 42M in Athlean — produces better outcomes than spreading capital thinly across 20 early-stage bets. One company you understand deeply is worth more than ten you don't.

tips.headline: Build Your Team Before You Build Your Fund
tips.body: A professional investment vehicle needs legal, financial, and deal-sourcing infrastructure before it can scale. Hire two to three specialists before you raise a single euro from outside LPs — the structure is what makes the capital work.

tips.headline: Pilot Deals Before You Commit Capital
tips.body: Before Haaland backed FieldIQ with equity, the platform piloted inside academies he had relationships with. Pilot agreements cost nothing and tell you whether a company can execute before you write a cheque.

tips.headline: Position Your Fund Raise as a Series, Not a One-Off
tips.body: Converting a personal vehicle into a fund with LP capital multiplies your firepower without requiring more of your own cash. Structure the raise in rounds — it signals professionalism and gives LPs a clear entry point.

investments.headline: Backs French Circular Sportswear Brand Renewd
investments.body: Antoine Griezmann's vehicle AG7 Capital co-led a €2.8M seed round in Renewd, a Lyon-based sportswear brand producing performance apparel from recycled ocean plastic. Griezmann cited growing athlete demand for sustainable kit options and Renewd's B2B pipeline — the company already supplies three Ligue 1 clubs with training kit. AG7 takes a board observer seat as part of the deal.

investments.headline: Invests in Slovenian Endurance Recovery Platform Recu
investments.body: Tour de France champion Tadej Pogačar made a personal €1.5M seed investment in Recu, a Ljubljana-based platform combining sleep tracking, load monitoring, and personalised recovery protocols for endurance athletes. Pogačar will join an athlete advisory board alongside two other professional cyclists. The company has 28,000 active users across 40 countries and is targeting a Series A in late 2026.

investments.headline: Joins Series A in European Player Development App ProShot
investments.body: ATP world No. 3 Daniil Medvedev participated in a €3.5M Series A for ProShot, an AI-powered video analysis platform for tennis coaches and junior players. ProShot uses pose estimation to generate technique feedback in real time, reducing the need for expensive coaching hours. Medvedev, who began his development in France, said he backed ProShot because affordable technical feedback would have accelerated his own junior career by years.
